name: Sant Cugat de Gavadons - circular gravel through Moianès
name_original: Sant Cugat de Gavadons - circular gravel por el Moianès
region_intro: Touring cycling around Moià, located in Catalonia, Spain, offers diverse landscapes transitioning from inland plains to pre-Pyrenean reliefs. The region features a varied topography with plateaus, valleys, and hills, alongside extensive beech, oak, Scots pine, and holm oak forests. Mosaic fields intersperse these wooded areas, creating a rich visual environment for cycling. This blend of natural features provides a range of terrain suitable for various touring cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Moià The most popular touring cycling route is Sant Cugat de Gavadons - circular gravel through Moianès, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours to complete, traversing diverse gravel terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Moià – Puig de la Caritat loop from Moià, a difficult 20.6 miles (33.2 km) path offering varied terrain and scenic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Ermita de Sant Sebastià – Rural Road to Sant Sebastià loop from Moià, a 36.6 miles (59.0 km) trail leading through rural roads and forests, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Touring cycling around Moià is defined by plateaus, valleys, and forests, with routes traversing rural roads and offering diverse landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more demanding expeditions. The routes in Moià are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Moià's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Moià?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Moià region, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Moià?
A: The majority of touring cycling routes in Moià are rated as difficult, with 27 out of 36 routes falling into this category. There are also 8 moderate routes and 1 easy route, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Moià?
A: Moià offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from inland plains to pre-Pyrenean reliefs. You'll cycle through plateaus, valleys, and hills, with extensive beech, oak, Scots pine, and holm oak forests, interspersed with picturesque mosaic fields. The region's topography provides a rich visual environment for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that feature waterfalls or natural monuments?
A: Yes, the Moià region is known for its impressive natural features. You can find routes that pass near waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2197382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Tosca Waterfall and Molí de Brotons&lt;/a&gt;, or the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/236272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Monastery and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a historical site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/809682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Road&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highly scenic cycleway.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Moià?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Moià are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976102433/sant-cugat-de-gavadons-circular-gravel-through-moianes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Cugat de Gavadons - circular gravel through Moianès&lt;/a&gt;, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31987860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moià – Puig de la Caritat loop from Moià&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23822855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Sebastià – Rural Road to Sant Sebastià loop from Moià&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Moià?
A: The touring cycling routes around Moià are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from gravel paths to rural roads, and the scenic views of plateaus, valleys, and forests. The variety of routes caters to different experiences, from leisurely rides to more demanding expeditions.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks I can visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history and culture. You can incorporate visits to traditional farmhouses, old mills, Romanesque hermitages, and historic cobbled paths into your tours. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/236272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Monastery and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical site with Gothic architecture and stalactite caves, directly accessible from a popular cycleway.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: While many routes are rated as difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available in Moià. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them more suitable for families or those new to touring cycling. Always check the route details for specific difficulty ratings and terrain before heading out.

Q: Can I find routes with good viewpoints in Moià?
A: Yes, the varied topography of Moià, with its hills and pre-Pyrenean reliefs, offers numerous opportunities for scenic viewpoints. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31904703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Collsuspina – Roure del Giol loop from Moià&lt;/a&gt; are specifically named for their panoramic vistas, allowing you to enjoy the expansive landscapes of the Moianès region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Moià feature a mix of terrain. You'll encounter gravel paths, rural roads, and sometimes historic cobbled paths. The region's transition from plains to pre-Pyrenean reliefs means you can expect varied surfaces and elevation changes, providing a dynamic cycling experience.

Q: Are there any caves to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the Moià area is home to several interesting caves. While cycling, you might be near natural wonders like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3015059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balma dels Gobians&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3498609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drac de Vilarjoan Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4577635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Balma d'en Xec&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/236272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Monastery and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; also features stalactite caves.
tips: The monastery of Santa María, listed as a historical-artistic monument in 1931, is located in the historic center of L'Estany and can be visited by booking through the website: https://entradas.codetickets.com/entradas/entrada-individual -al-monestir-de-santa-maria-de-l-estany/20011/cases-oliba/online. Above all, the cloister stands out, whose gallery of arches crowned by capitals is one of the best preserved from the Catalan Romanesque period.
Small observatory in the Serrado de Hook. Right on the livestock path of Fontfreda
It is a medium-sized church from the 12th century. It doesn't appear to be in use, but it's nice to visit or take a photo.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Santa María de l'Estany
